characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The host of the show. Game Show Host: The title says it all. Large Ham: What's Ruff without this? Insistent Terminology: As established in the first episode, he doesn't like it when Studio G is called a garage.Ruff:All right, rule number one! Weneverrefer to Studio G as "the garage", okay? It makes me a little insecure. Trademark Favorite Food: Chinese food is Ruff's favorite thing to eat.

characters.name: Ruff Ruffman
characters.description: The host of the show. Game Show Host: The title says it all. Large Ham: What's Ruff without this? Insistent Terminology: As established in the first episode, he doesn't like it when Studio G is called a garage.Ruff:All right, rule number one! Weneverrefer to Studio G as "the garage", okay? It makes me a little insecure. Trademark Favorite Food: Chinese food is Ruff's favorite thing to eat.

characters.name: Scruff Ruffman
characters.description: Evil Twin: Is this to Ruff. Noodle Incident: We never learn how or why he ended up in prison.

characters.name: Grandma Ruffman
characters.description: Ambiguously Jewish: Nothing is mentioned about her exact religion, but she has a Yiddish accent and calls Ruff "bubby" (a diminutive of "bubala", a Yiddish term of affection).

characters.name: Glen
characters.description: Ambiguously Related: Downplayed as he is explicitly Ruff and Scruff's nephew, but whether he is Roxy's son or the son of another unidentified Ruffman sibling in Ruff's generation is never revealed. Pubescent Braces: Wears braces, making it hard for Ruff to understand what he is saying at times.

characters.name: Ruffael Ruffmanowitz
characters.description: Posthumous Character: Because of the 6 times great grandfather relationship to Ruff, he is only mentioned.

characters.name: Roxy Ruffman
characters.description: He Who Must Not Be Seen: She is never shown, only mentioned.

characters.name: Charlene
characters.description: Satellite Love Interest: We only know that Ruff wants to be with her, and not much else.

characters.name: Spot Spotnik
characters.description: The Rival: Ruff's rival who constantly tries to one up him, and get with Charlene. Wardrobe Malfunction: In the end of one episode, Spot's pants disintegrate leaving his bottom half exposed in public.

characters.name: Blossom
characters.description: Cats Are Snarkers: Or rather,SilentSnarkers: Blossom doesn't speak, but her actions scream sarcastic. Hyper-Competent Sidekick: Before her promotion she's a highly skilled assistant, with knowledge of any topic relevant to the episode. She's also a skilled acrobat. Not So Stoic: She's normally aSilent Snarkerwho doesn't express much emotion, but when Marc compares Ruff's girth to that of a beluga whale, she can't stop herself from full-on laughing at the comment.It is incredibly precious. Overly Long Name: Her actual name is 'Princess Blossom Pepper Doodle Van Yum Yum.' Smart People Wear Glasses: She wears glasses and is shown to be much more intelligent than Ruff or Chet.

characters.name: Anna
characters.description: Odd Name Out: The only season one contestant who has a name starting with a vowel instead of a consonant.

characters.name: Khalil
characters.description: Pronouncing My Name for You:Discussed. He jokes about the fact that some people don't pronounce his name right and having to correct them during a stand-up comedy routine.

characters.name: Brian
characters.description: Freudian Excuse: He is scared of bees due to being stung by one when he was 3 years old. Why Did It Have to Be Snakes?: He wasn't thrilled about the challenge in which he and Khalil learn about bees due to being scared of them.

characters.name: Rosario
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: Reappeared in the season 3 episode "The People Versus Grandma Ruffman" as the foreman of the jury in Grandma Ruffman's trial.

characters.name: Madi
characters.description: My Nayme Is: Spells her nickname as "Madi" instead of "Maddie".

characters.name: Jay
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: One of the snarkier contestants of the series.

characters.name: Noel
characters.description: The Cheerleader: Her disguise in the spy episode is a cheerleader known as "Lila Splits" and she's also a member of her cheerleading team. Token Minority: The only Asian contestant of the season.

characters.name: DJ
characters.description: Only Known by Their Nickname: His first name, Demetrius, is only said once on the show due to him being called by his first two initials most of the time.

characters.name: Isaac
characters.description: Odd Name Out: His name starts with a vowel instead of a consonant like his fellow contestants' names.

characters.name: Talia
characters.description: Fangirl: Of Roger Federer. Upon learning she's touching a tennis racket he's held before, she starts screaming in excitement.

characters.name: Marco
characters.description: Earn Your Happy Ending: Marco was in last place by the last episode and one could be forgiven for thinking that he wouldn't win; he overcame the odds to become the final winner of the series though.

characters.name: Emmie
characters.description: Graceful Loser: She gave Marco a hug when he won. Odd Name Out: Like Anna and Isaac before her, she has a name that starts with a vowel instead of a consonant like her fellow contestants.

characters.name: Rubye
characters.description: My Nayme Is: Her name is pronounced like "Ruby" despite an extra letter at the end. Trademark Favorite Food: She is thrilled to go out on a challenge in which she learns how to make cheese due to it being her favorite food.
